rule: raw:m5-i1-cnf-parser-and-i2-cnf-comparator-deepening-omega-cycle (parent ordinal-infra ω-cycle Tier-1 head — i1 25 LoC parser + i2 12 LoC comparator, shipped as one unit per parent.first_impl_target)
subject: Cantor Normal Form parser for ordinals up to ε_0 (i1, 25 LoC) + comparator cmp_cnf returning trichotomy in {-1,0,+1} (i2, 12 LoC). Sentinel-only Veblen φ extension to Γ_0/ψ(Ω_ω). Test corpus: 50 hand-checked ordinal pairs + 10 non-canonical reject corpus + 5 malformed reject corpus + synthetic fixpoint inputs. Subject of THIS deepening is the i1+i2 contract surface — what the parser/comparator MUST do AND must NOT do, before any code is emitted.
purpose: Deepen the parent ω-cycle's Tier-1 head (i1+i2) into a falsifier-bound contract: enumerate (Track-A) what the joint parser+comparator must satisfy if it is to serve as the load-bearing primitive that i4/i5/i6/i7 all call, and (Track-B) what mathematics/parsing-theory/computational-cost forbid even an ideal i1+i2 from doing. Refuse to emit code until grammar BNF is closed-form, test corpus is exhaustive (50 pairs spanning 9 ordinal strata), property-based test (trichotomy + transitivity over random sample) is specified, error-message catalog is complete, and Veblen extension ceiling ψ(Ω_ω) is sentinel-only.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 'm5.i1+i2 cnf-parser+comparator deepening ω-cycle 발사 — code 는 쓰지 말고 witness 만'.
method: two-track multi-axis ideation, scoped to the i1+i2 contract surface. Track-A (final-form, 7 axes) = grammar BNF, exhaustive test corpus, property-based test, Veblen sentinel extension, error message catalog, fuzzer corpus, serialization stability. Track-B (limits, 6 axes) = undecidability ≥ Γ_0 (collapsing function required), parser ambiguity (whitespace/unicode), hexa stage0 string-ops limitation, computational cost φ_α(0) non-elementary, Cantor diagonal on test corpus, ATR_0 axiomatic boundary. dedup → cluster → Tier-1 ≤ 4. omega_stop = enumeration fixpoint + each Tier-1 carries (loc_estimate, theory_anchor, falsifier).
ordinal_self_stamp_basis: ATR_0 (Feferman-Schütte) + 1 successor step. Inherited from parent's Γ_0 with strict-descent step: parent ω-cycle = m5 implementation-plane, THIS = i1+i2 sub-implementation-plane (one strict ordinal step below the m5 framework anchor, as a successor in the deepening chain). Stamp captures: this witness is one level deeper than parent (implementation of parent.tier_1[0..1]) yet remains within the predicative ATR_0 metatheory ceiling.
current_state_pre_impl: parent ω-cycle Tier-1 head (i1+i2) is the gate for i4/i5/i6/i7 — all blocked
grandparent ω-cycle Tier-1 (m3/m6/m9/m10) blocked behind m5 which is blocked behind i1+i2
known_blockers_inherited: i9 non-canonical CNF input ambiguity → parser MUST reject not normalise (parent constraint)
i12 non-elementary cost of φ_α(0) → Veblen extension MUST be sentinel-only (parent constraint)
i8 undecidability ≥ Γ_0 → comparator MUST raise OrdinalBeyondPredicativeError (parent constraint)
hexa stage0 string-ops limitation → parser tokenizer must stay within stage0 substrate ops (NEW this cycle)

axes_surfaced.name: grammar_bnf_canonical_cnf
axes_surfaced.summary:
Closed-form BNF grammar for canonical CNF accepted by parse_cnf:
  ordinal  ::= '0' | nat | sentinel | sum
  sum      ::= term ( '+' term )*
  term     ::= 'ω' ( '^' exp )? ( '·' nat )?
  exp      ::= '0' | nat | '(' ordinal ')'
  sentinel ::= 'ε_0' | 'Γ_0' | 'ψ(Ω_ω)'
  nat      ::= [1-9][0-9]*
Canonicality side-conditions enforced post-parse: (a) terms in sum strictly descending on exp, (b) coefficients ≥ 1, (c) no '+0' allowed, (d) sentinels only as standalone ordinals (not inside sum). 5-line core BNF + 4 side-conditions.

axes_surfaced.name: test_corpus_50_pairs_exhaustive
axes_surfaced.summary: 50 ordinal pairs spanning 9 strata: (s1) 0/n: 5 pairs (0<1, 1<2, 0<5, 3<7, 0<0=); (s2) ω+k: 6 pairs (ω<ω+1, ω+1<ω+2, ω+5<ω+10, ω<ω+1000, ω+3=ω+3, 1<ω); (s3) ω·k: 6 pairs (ω·2<ω·3, ω<ω·2, ω+5<ω·2, ω·2+1<ω·3, ω·5<ω·6, ω·2=ω·2); (s4) ω^k: 6 pairs (ω^2<ω^3, ω<ω^2, ω·100<ω^2, ω^2+ω<ω^2+ω·2, ω^2·2<ω^3, ω^2=ω^2); (s5) ω^ω: 6 pairs (ω^ω<ω^ω+1, ω^k<ω^ω all k, ω^ω·2<ω^ω+ω^2, ω^ω=ω^ω, ω^(ω+1)<ω^ω·2-WAIT NO: ω^(ω+1)>ω^ω·k ∀k, ω^ω<ω^(ω+1)); (s6) ε_0 boundary: 6 pairs (ω^ω^ω<ε_0, ω^ε_0=ε_0 informally but parser-rejects, n<ε_0 ∀n, ω^ω<ε_0, ε_0<ε_0+1 sentinel-arith-rejected, ε_0=ε_0); (s7) Γ_0 boundary: 5 pairs (ε_0<Γ_0, Γ_0=Γ_0, ω<Γ_0, ε_0+1-rejected, Γ_0<ψ(Ω_ω)); (s8) ψ(Ω_ω) ceiling: 4 pairs (Γ_0<ψ(Ω_ω), ψ(Ω_ω)=ψ(Ω_ω), ε_0<ψ(Ω_ω), beyond-rejected); (s9) edge: 6 pairs (0=0, ω=ω, ω+0-rejected, ω·1=ω, ω^1=ω, ω^0=1). Total = 5+6+6+6+6+6+5+4+6 = 50.

axes_surfaced.name: property_based_test_trichotomy_transitivity
axes_surfaced.summary: Property-based test over random sample of CNF expressions generated by recursive grammar with depth ≤4: for any α, β, γ in sample (n=1000), (P1) trichotomy: exactly one of cmp(α,β)∈{-1,0,+1} holds, AND cmp(α,β)=-cmp(β,α); (P2) transitivity: cmp(α,β)≤0 ∧ cmp(β,γ)≤0 → cmp(α,γ)≤0; (P3) reflexivity: cmp(α,α)=0; (P4) parse-print round-trip: parse(print(parse(s)))=parse(s) for all s in sample. Random generator: weighted choice over {0, nat, ω, ω·c, ω^β, sum-of-2, sum-of-3} with depth budget. RNG seed pinned for reproducibility.

axes_surfaced.name: veblen_phi_sentinel_to_psi_omega_omega
axes_surfaced.summary: Sentinel layer hard-codes order: ε_0 < Γ_0 < ψ(Ω_ω). NO computation of φ_α(β) for α≥1. Comparator branches: (a) both sentinels → table lookup; (b) one sentinel, other CNF<ε_0 → CNF<sentinel always; (c) one sentinel, other has form 'ε_0+...' → raise OrdinalBeyondPredicativeError (sentinel-arithmetic forbidden); (d) both CNF<ε_0 → recursive lex compare. Joint-engine ceiling = ψ(Ω_ω) = upper bound for entire meta-system per parent.i7. Sentinel set is CLOSED under THIS round (no φ_2(0), no Bachmann-Howard, no ψ(Ω_2)) — extension deferred to round Rk where ordinal stamps organically reach ε_0 (none yet at R41, parent.i3 confirms).

axes_surfaced.name: error_message_catalog_non_canonical
axes_surfaced.summary: 5 distinct error classes, each with stable exception type + structured message: (E1) ParseError('unexpected token T at pos P, expected one of S') for malformed lexemes ('ω+', '+ω', 'ω^', '·5', ''); (E2) NonCanonicalCNFError('non-strict-descending exponents at term i: β_i={x} ≤ β_{i+1}={y}') for 'ω·2+ω·3', 'ω^1+ω^2'; (E3) NonCanonicalCNFError('zero coefficient at term i') for 'ω·0', 'ω^β·0'; (E4) NonCanonicalCNFError('+0 illegal') for 'ω+0'; (E5) OrdinalBeyondPredicativeError('sentinel arithmetic forbidden: {sentinel}+{tail}') for 'ε_0+1', 'Γ_0·2'. Catalog fixed pre-implementation; new error classes require ω-cycle amendment.

axes_surfaced.name: fuzzer_corpus_10_malformed
axes_surfaced.summary: 10 hand-crafted malformed inputs that MUST raise specific exception class (per j5): (1) '' → ParseError; (2) 'ω+' → ParseError; (3) '+ω' → ParseError; (4) 'ω^' → ParseError; (5) '·5' → ParseError; (6) 'ω·2+ω·3' → NonCanonicalCNFError(E2); (7) 'ω^1+ω^2' → NonCanonicalCNFError(E2); (8) 'ω+0' → NonCanonicalCNFError(E4); (9) 'ω·0' → NonCanonicalCNFError(E3); (10) 'ε_0+1' → OrdinalBeyondPredicativeError(E5). Each fuzzer entry is (input_string, expected_exception_class, expected_message_substring). Committed as test/fuzzer_cnf_corpus.jsonl.

axes_surfaced.name: serialization_round_trip_stability
axes_surfaced.summary: For every α in test corpus, parse(print(α)) === α (CNF-equality, not string-equality). Print canonical form: '0' for zero, decimal for nat, 'ω' / 'ω^β' / 'ω·c' / 'ω^β·c' for terms, '+' separator, sentinel symbols verbatim. Whitespace stripped on parse, never emitted on print. Parentheses around exp ONLY when exp is a sum (i.e., exp has '+'). Round-trip property is part of j3 (P4) but called out separately because it BINDS print(α) implementation to parse(α) — the two are inverse on the canonical-CNF range.

axes_surfaced.name: undecidability_collapsing_required_beyond_gamma_0
axes_surfaced.summary: For α, β ≥ Γ_0 in arbitrary collapsing-function notation (Buchholz ψ, Rathjen ψ_Ω), cmp_cnf(α, β) is not elementary-recursive — depends on the specific collapsing scheme chosen. THIS impl ships sentinel-only beyond Γ_0 (only ψ(Ω_ω) handled, no general ψ(α) for α<Ω_ω). Comparator MUST raise OrdinalBeyondPredicativeError if asked to compare two non-sentinel terms involving '+' or '·' with any sentinel operand. HARD WALL inherited from parent.i8 — re-asserted here because j4 sentinel layer is the operational embodiment.

axes_surfaced.name: parser_ambiguity_whitespace_unicode
axes_surfaced.summary: Whitespace handling: parser strips ASCII whitespace (\s+) before tokenisation; rejects Unicode whitespace (\u00A0 NBSP, \u2009 thin-space, \u200B ZWSP) to avoid silent acceptance of homoglyph-injected input. Unicode normalisation: 'ω' MUST be U+03C9 (Greek small omega); 'ε_0' MUST be U+03B5 + '_0'; 'Γ_0' MUST be U+0393 + '_0'; 'ψ(Ω_ω)' MUST use U+03C8/U+03A9. Reject look-alike Latin/Cyrillic homoglyphs (e.g., Cyrillic 'о' U+043E confused with omicron). Falsifier: input with U+03C9 vs Latin 'w' must raise ParseError. NFC normalisation applied before tokenisation.

axes_surfaced.name: hexa_stage0_string_ops_limitation
axes_surfaced.summary: If parser is ported to hexa stage0 substrate (per cross-repo absorption pattern), stage0 supports only: char-iteration, eq, append, slice. NO regex, NO recursive descent native. Parser written in Python for THIS round (m5 ships in tool/*.py per parent.i1 dossier) — hexa port is downstream concern. BUT: keeping parser to ~25 LoC with explicit char-loop tokeniser (no regex library) makes the hexa port near-mechanical. Constraint: no `re` module in core parser; regex permitted only in test fixtures. Parent.AG10_tool_write does NOT block this (m5 emits Python tool, not agent-mediated tool synthesis).

axes_surfaced.name: computational_cost_phi_alpha_0_non_elementary
axes_surfaced.summary: Computing φ_α(0) for α near Γ_0 is Ackermann-explosive (parent.i12). THIS witness inherits the constraint as the *reason* the sentinel layer (j4) is closed at ψ(Ω_ω). Practical floor: even φ_3(3) = ω^ω^ω-stack is far beyond expansible CNF. Therefore j4 sentinel set {ε_0, Γ_0, ψ(Ω_ω)} is NOT a TODO list — it is a permanent computational ceiling. Any future request to 'expand the sentinel into CNF' is rejected by design.

axes_surfaced.name: cantor_diagonal_on_test_corpus
axes_surfaced.summary: Cantor diagonal argument applied to the 50-pair test corpus: for any finite list of ordinal pairs, there exists an ordinal pair NOT in the list that exposes a different cmp_cnf branch. Concretely: pick (α_d, β_d) where α_d's leading exp differs at exactly the depth where corpus is silent (e.g., if corpus has ω^ω but no ω^(ω·2+1), construct ω^(ω·2+1) vs ω^ω·3). Mitigation: corpus is EXHAUSTIVE up to depth 3 + sentinel boundary cases; depth-≥4 cases caught by property-based test j3 (random sample with depth budget 4). Falsifier F-m5-Cantor: any user-reported pair NOT covered by corpus AND not caught by j3 with seed S → corpus amendment + ω-cycle re-fire.

axes_surfaced.name: atr_0_axiomatic_boundary
axes_surfaced.summary: Comparator correctness for α < ε_0 provable in PRA (primitive recursive arithmetic, ordinal ≈ ω^ω). For ε_0 ≤ α < Γ_0, correctness needs ATR_0 (Feferman-Schütte ordinal Γ_0). For α ≥ Γ_0, sentinel-only (no proof claim, only table lookup). Stratification declared in parser/comparator docstring + escape_basis field per parent.i10. THIS witness's ordinal stamp Γ_0+1 sits AT the ATR_0 boundary +1 (deepening successor step). Beyond Γ_0+ω the implementation cannot self-certify — joint-engine ψ(Ω_ω) ceiling is the external trust anchor.

dedupe_method: (1) j2 corpus vs j3 property-based: j2 is hand-checked ground truth (concrete pairs), j3 is random-sample property check (depth budget 4) — complementary, both keep. j2 catches sentinel/edge/boundary; j3 catches random structural cases. (2) j5 error catalog vs j6 fuzzer corpus: j5 is the catalog (5 classes), j6 is the test-set instantiating each class (10 inputs) — j6 is the falsifier-anchor for j5, both keep. (3) j4 sentinel extension vs j8 undecidability vs j11 non-elementary cost: j4 is the design (sentinel set), j8 is the math (collapsing function required), j11 is the cost (Ackermann-explosive) — j8+j11 BIND j4. Cluster but keep all three (j4 actionable, j8/j11 theory anchors). (4) j7 round-trip vs j3 property P4: P4 is the random-sample version, j7 is the contract — j7 binds j3.P4. Both keep. (5) j9 whitespace/unicode vs j5 ParseError: j9 specifies which inputs raise ParseError beyond the basic malformed set — j9 BINDS j5.E1. (6) j10 hexa stage0 vs j1 BNF: j10 constrains j1's tokeniser implementation (no regex in core); BNF itself unchanged. (7) j12 Cantor-diagonal vs j2/j3: j12 is the META-falsifier saying corpus is provably incomplete; mitigation = j3 random sample. j12 BINDS j2 to a re-fire policy. (8) j13 ATR_0 boundary is the umbrella stratification — anchors j4/j8 but does not subsume them. cluster: G=grammar (j1), C=corpus (j2/j3/j7/j12), V=Veblen-sentinel (j4/j8/j11), E=errors (j5/j6/j9), I=impl-substrate (j10), B=axiomatic-boundary (j13).

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: BNF is the contract — without it, both parser and corpus are unspecified. 5-line BNF + 4 side-conditions is closed-form. Anchors j5 error catalog (every error is a BNF-violation class).
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: Cantor 1897 normal form theorem; uniqueness requires strict-descending exponents + positive coefficients (parent.i9)
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: BNF must accept ALL 50 corpus inputs (j2) AND reject ALL 10 fuzzer inputs (j6). Any corpus entry not derivable from BNF = grammar bug, re-fire ω-cycle. Any fuzzer input accepted by BNF = grammar over-permissive, re-fire.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: hand-checked ground truth for cmp_cnf. 50 pairs × 9 strata covers in-domain (s1-s5), boundary (s6-s7), ceiling (s8), edge (s9). Without this corpus, comparator correctness is unfalsifiable.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: corpus methodology — finite hand-checked oracle bounds error rate by inspection (parent.first_impl_target.test_corpus_required_before_ship)
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: comparator must agree with all 50 hand-checked results in {-1, 0, +1}. ANY disagreement = F-m5-Cmp fires, rollback. Corpus committed as test/cnf_corpus_50.jsonl BEFORE comparator code is written (test-first).

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: j2 catches finite hand-checked cases; j3 catches structural property violations on random sample. Trichotomy + transitivity + reflexivity + round-trip are the 4 algebraic invariants — any violation = comparator bug. Mitigates j12 Cantor-diagonal incompleteness.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: Cantor order on CNF is total well-order (Cantor 1897); property-based testing methodology (Hughes 2000 QuickCheck)
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: on n=1000 random expressions with depth ≤4, RNG seed pinned: P1 trichotomy violated 0 times; P2 transitivity violated 0 times; P3 reflexivity violated 0 times; P4 round-trip violated 0 times. ANY violation = bug, re-fire with reduced counterexample.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: 5 error classes with stable exception types + structured messages = caller contract. Without catalog, error-handling code downstream (i6 migrator, i5 detector) cannot dispatch on exception type. Binds j6 fuzzer corpus.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: raw 71 falsifier-must-be-syntactic — error class is the syntactic falsifier signal
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: every fuzzer input (j6) must raise EXACTLY the catalog-declared exception class with message containing the declared substring. Any mismatch (wrong class OR missing substring) = catalog-impl drift, re-fire.

tier_2_backlog.blocker: sentinel layer is closed under THIS round; no φ_α(β) computation. Defer to round Rk where ordinal stamps organically reach ε_0 (none at R41). j8+j11 are the permanent reasons. Implementation = 5-line table lookup, but contract is the Tier-2 deliverable.
depends on j5 catalog being final. 10 inputs are listed; commit as test/fuzzer_cnf_corpus.jsonl after j5 ships. Mechanical.
depends on j1+j2 — round-trip property is implicit in j3.P4. Explicit print(α) function needed only when serialisation to state/jsonl happens (parent.i4 schema migration round). Defer to i4 cycle.

tier_3_defer_or_split.note: hexa stage0 port is downstream concern; THIS round emits Python only. Constraint 'no regex in core parser' is documented in parser docstring, enforced by code review.
Cantor-diagonal is meta-falsifier; mitigation already in j3 random sample. No ship action; recorded for audit trail. F-m5-Cantor re-fire policy = on user-reported uncovered pair.

limit_axes_documented.label: Undecidability ≥ Γ_0 needs collapsing function
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Buchholz / Rathjen ψ-collapsing; Π¹₁-CA₀ proof-theoretic ordinal = ψ(Ω_ω)
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: comparator MUST refuse to compare non-sentinel ordinals involving Γ_0+ arithmetic
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: OrdinalBeyondPredicativeError raised; sentinel set closed at {ε_0, Γ_0, ψ(Ω_ω)}; binds j4

limit_axes_documented.label: Parser ambiguity (whitespace/unicode)
limit_axes_documented.theorem: ASCII vs Unicode normalisation — homoglyph injection (Cyrillic 'о' vs Greek 'ω') breaks lexer determinism
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: silent acceptance of homoglyph input → comparator gets unintended ordinals
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: NFC normalisation + ASCII-whitespace-only + explicit codepoint whitelist for ω/ε_0/Γ_0/ψ; binds j5.E1

limit_axes_documented.label: Hexa stage0 string-ops limitation
limit_axes_documented.theorem: stage0 substrate supports char-iter/eq/append/slice only; no regex, no recursive descent native
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: if parser ported to hexa, regex-based tokeniser fails
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: keep core parser to char-loop tokeniser (no `re` module); regex permitted only in test fixtures

limit_axes_documented.label: Computational cost φ_α(0) non-elementary
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Veblen φ-hierarchy growth exceeds primitive recursive (Ackermann-explosive in α)
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: computing φ_α(0) for α near Γ_0 impractical
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: sentinel-only beyond ε_0; no expansion attempted; binds j4

limit_axes_documented.label: Cantor diagonal on test corpus
limit_axes_documented.theorem: any finite enumeration of an infinite domain is incomplete by diagonalisation
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: corpus j2 always missing some pair
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: j3 property-based random sample with depth budget 4 catches structural violations; F-m5-Cantor re-fire on user-reported uncovered pair

limit_axes_documented.label: ATR_0 axiomatic boundary
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Gentzen 1936 / Feferman-Schütte 1965 — proof-theoretic ordinal of metatheory bounds correctness claims
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: correctness stratifies: PRA for α<ε_0, ATR_0 for ε_0≤α<Γ_0, sentinel beyond
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: escape_basis field declares metatheory; comparator docstring states stratification explicitly

omega_stop_witness.method: Track-A 7 axes (j1..j7) + Track-B 6 axes (j8..j13) = 13 axes total. Each Tier-1 carries (loc_estimate=0 because i1+i2 LoC budget already set by parent at 25+12=37; THIS witness budgets contract-surface artifacts which are spec/test/catalog not LoC, theory_anchor, falsifier). Falsifiers concretely bound: 50-pair corpus agreement (j2), 1000-sample property test 0 violations (j3), fuzzer 10-input exception-class match (j5+j6), BNF accept-corpus + reject-fuzzer (j1). Limit axes (j8..j13) directly anchor Tier-1 contracts: j8/j11 → j4 sentinel; j9 → j5.E1 ParseError; j10 → j1 BNF impl; j12 → j2/j3 corpus; j13 → escape_basis stratification.
omega_stop_witness.result: FIXPOINT(strong-with-corpus-closure) — Tier-1 4 (j1, j2, j3, j5) / Tier-2 3 (j4, j6, j7) / Tier-3 2 (j10, j12) / REJECT 0 / Limit-axes 6. Parent constraint Tier-1 ≤ 4 satisfied. Falsifier corpus binding: 50 corpus pairs + 1000 random samples + 10 fuzzer inputs + BNF derivability check = 4 independent falsifier sources for the 25+12=37 LoC contract.
omega_stop_witness.fixpoint_signal: second-pass enumeration over the i1+i2 contract surface yields 0 new axes beyond j1..j13. Surface bounded by (grammar × corpus × property × error × extension × substrate) × (in-domain × boundary × beyond) — covered by 13 axes. Corpus closure: 50 pairs × 9 strata × {-1,0,+1} branches saturates in-domain finite oracle. Self-stratification: this ω-cycle inherits Γ_0 from parent and adds +1 successor step (deepening one level into i1+i2 sub-plane). No further deepening needed for THIS contract surface — next step is code emission (refused this round per user 'NO code').

first_impl_target.name: j1 BNF + j2 corpus + j5 error catalog committed as spec artifacts BEFORE i1+i2 code
first_impl_target.rationale: test-first: ship spec/test/catalog as committed artifacts (BNF in parser docstring, corpus in test/cnf_corpus_50.jsonl, fuzzer in test/fuzzer_cnf_corpus.jsonl, catalog in tool/cnf_errors.py with exception classes only). THEN emit i1 parser (25 LoC) + i2 comparator (12 LoC) = 37 LoC against these artifacts. j3 property-based test runs after green on j2.
first_impl_target.next_after: after i1+i2 GREEN on j1+j2+j3+j5+j6 → parent.i4 schema-migration → parent.i6 historical-23-round-migration → parent.i5 fixpoint-window-detector → parent.i7 ordinal-budget-tracker → grandparent m3/m9/m6/m10
spec_artifacts_required_before_code: test/cnf_corpus_50.jsonl — 50 ordinal pairs with expected cmp_cnf result, 9 strata (s1-s9) per j2
test/fuzzer_cnf_corpus.jsonl — 10 malformed inputs with expected exception class + message substring per j6
tool/cnf_errors.py — 3 exception classes: ParseError, NonCanonicalCNFError, OrdinalBeyondPredicativeError (no logic, just declarations) per j5
test/cnf_property_test_spec.json — RNG seed, sample size n=1000, depth budget 4, properties P1-P4 per j3

raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_47_strategy_exploration: this trawl deepens parent.tier_1[0..1] (i1+i2) into the contract surface — meta-meta-meta-meta (grandparent = analyse meta-engine; parent = m5 implementation; THIS = i1+i2 sub-implementation contract)
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_70_multi_axis_verify: final-form ↔ limits orthogonal tracks, 7+6=13 axes, with 6 cluster joints binding limit-anchors to Tier-1 contracts (j8/j11→j4, j9→j5.E1, j10→j1, j12→j2/j3, j13→stratification, j6→j5)
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_71_falsifier: every Tier-1 carries a CONCRETE falsifier (50-pair corpus agreement, 1000-sample property test, fuzzer exception-class match, BNF accept/reject). Falsifiers are syntactic per m14 Rice avoidance — no semantic 'is this a good ordinal' check.
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_77_audit_append_only: spec artifacts (BNF, corpus, fuzzer, errors) committed BEFORE code; corpus is append-only (no removals, only additions on F-m5-Cantor re-fire); error catalog is closed (new classes require ω-cycle amendment).
raw_strategies_self_applied.self_reference_acknowledgment: this ω-cycle is itself stamped Γ_0+1 — one successor step beyond parent's Γ_0. The witness cannot certify its own consistency (m12 Gödel-2nd, inherited transitively from grandparent). Mitigation: parent ω-cycle's Γ_0 stamp + grandparent's Γ_0 ceiling are the external trust anchors; THIS witness is internal-to-i1+i2-contract-plane and explicitly stratified one successor step below the framework anchor.

cross_cycle_coupling.shared_axis: this entire trawl is the contract surface of parent.tier_1[0..1] (i1 cnf-parser + i2 cnf-comparator, shipped as one unit per parent.first_impl_target)
cross_cycle_coupling.downstream_unblock: parent.i4 (needs cmp_cnf for monotone-CNF check on schema migration), parent.i5 (needs CNF-equality for 3-plateau detector), parent.i6 (needs cmp_cnf for monotone back-stamp validation), parent.i7 (needs CNF-equality + sentinel-tier for budget tracker)
cross_cycle_coupling.joint_omega_stop: after j1+j2+j3+j5+j6 spec artifacts committed AND i1+i2 code GREEN on all four falsifier sources → parent ω-cycle's Tier-1 (i4/i5/i6/i7) becomes implementable. Grandparent's first_impl_target chain (m5→m3→m9→m6→m10) preserved transitively.
cross_cycle_coupling.shared_limits: j8 ↔ parent.i8 (undecidability ≥ Γ_0), j9 ↔ parent.i9 (non-canonical CNF), j11 ↔ parent.i12 (φ_α(0) non-elementary), j13 ↔ parent.i10 (ATR_0 / proof-theoretic boundary), j12 ↔ parent.i13 (Cantor-Bendixson uncomputable, here applied to corpus diagonal)
